questions.text: What is the measure of a right angle?
questions.explanation: A right angle measures exactly 90 degrees.

questions.text: Two angles whose sum is 90° are called:
questions.explanation: Complementary angles add up to 90 degrees.

questions.text: If two lines intersect, the angles opposite to each other are called:
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal and opposite each other at the intersection.

questions.text: The sum of angles on a straight line is:
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180 degrees.

questions.text: Which pair of angles are adjacent?

options.a: Two angles with a common vertex and a common arm
options.c: Two angles opposite each other at intersection
options.d: Two angles inside parallel lines on opposite sides of transversal

questions.explanation: Adjacent angles share a common vertex and a common arm but do not overlap.

questions.text: Two angles that add up to 180° are called:
questions.explanation: Supplementary angles sum to 180 degrees.

questions.text: If a transversal cuts two parallel lines, the angles on the same side of the transversal inside the parallel lines are called:
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles lie on the same side of the transversal and inside the parallel lines.

questions.text: Corresponding angles are:

options.a: Equal when the lines are parallel

questions.explanation: Corresponding angles are equal when the transversal cuts parallel lines.

questions.text: Alternate interior angles lie:

options.a: On the same side of the transversal and inside the parallel lines
options.b: On opposite sides of the transversal and inside the parallel lines
options.c: On the same side of the transversal and outside the parallel lines
options.d: On opposite sides of the transversal and outside the parallel lines

questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles are on opposite sides of the transversal and inside the parallel lines.

questions.text: Which of the following is a condition for two lines to be parallel?
questions.explanation: If corresponding angles are equal, the two lines are parallel.

questions.text: If two angles are adjacent and their sum is 180°, they are:
questions.explanation: Adjacent angles summing to 180° are supplementary adjacent angles.

questions.text: What is the measure of a straight angle?
questions.explanation: A straight angle measures 180 degrees.

questions.text: If two lines intersect, how many pairs of vertically opposite angles are formed?
questions.explanation: Two pairs of vertically opposite angles are formed when two lines intersect.

questions.text: Which of the following pairs of angles are always equal?
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are always equal.

questions.text: If a transversal cuts two lines and alternate interior angles are equal, then:
questions.explanation: Equal alternate interior angles indicate the lines are parallel.

questions.text: If two angles are complementary and one angle is 35°, what is the other angle?
questions.explanation: Complementary angles sum to 90°, so 90° - 35° = 55° (correct answer is 55°, option a is incorrect). Correction: The correct answer is 55°, so option a should be correct. Adjusting options accordingly.

questions.text: When a transversal cuts two parallel lines, the sum of co-interior angles on the same side of the transversal is:
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles on the same side of the transversal sum to 180° when lines are parallel.

questions.text: If two adjacent angles form a right angle, then they are:
questions.explanation: Adjacent angles summing to 90° are complementary.

questions.text: In the figure, if two lines are cut by a transversal and corresponding angles are not equal, then:
questions.explanation: Non-equal corresponding angles indicate the lines are not parallel.

questions.text: If the sum of two adjacent angles is 180°, what can be said about these angles?

options.b: They are supplementary and form a straight line

questions.explanation: Adjacent angles summing to 180° are supplementary and lie on a straight line.

questions.text: A ladder leans against a wall making an angle of 60° with the ground. The wall and the ground are perpendicular. What is the angle between the ladder and the wall?
questions.explanation: The wall and ground form a right angle (90°). The ladder makes 60° with the ground, so angle between ladder and wall = 90° - 60° = 30°.

questions.text: Two adjacent angles on a straight line are in the ratio 2:3. Find the measures of these angles.
questions.explanation: Sum of adjacent angles on a straight line = 180°. Let angles be 2x and 3x. Then 2x + 3x = 180 ⇒ 5x = 180 ⇒ x = 36. Angles are 72° and 108°.

questions.text: A transversal cuts two parallel railway tracks, making an angle of 70° with one track. What is the measure of the alternate interior angle on the other track?
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles formed by a transversal cutting parallel lines are equal. Hence, the alternate interior angle is 70°.

questions.text: In a park, two paths intersect at a point forming vertically opposite angles. If one angle is 65°, find the measure of its vertically opposite angle.
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal. Therefore, the opposite angle is also 65°.

questions.text: Two angles are complementary. If one angle is 35°, what is the measure of the other angle?
questions.explanation: Complementary angles sum to 90°. Other angle = 90° - 35° = 55°.

questions.text: A transversal intersects two parallel roads. If one of the corresponding angles is 120°, find the measure of the co-interior angle on the same side of the transversal.
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles on the same side of the transversal sum to 180°. So, co-interior angle = 180° - 120° = 60°.

questions.text: A rectangular field has a corner angle of 90°. A fence is built inside the field making an angle of 30° with one side. What is the angle between the fence and the adjacent side?
questions.explanation: Since the corner is 90°, the adjacent angle is 90° - 30° = 60°.

questions.text: Two angles are supplementary and one is 25° more than the other. Find the measures of the two angles.
questions.explanation: Let angles be x and x+25. Their sum is 180°, so 2x + 25 = 180 ⇒ 2x = 155 ⇒ x = 77.5°. Angles are 77.5° and 102.5°.

questions.text: A transversal cuts two parallel lines. If one angle is 55°, find all other angles formed by the transversal with the lines.
questions.explanation: Corresponding and alternate interior angles equal 55°, co-interior angles equal 125°. So angles are 55°, 125°, 55°, 125°.

questions.text: Two adjacent angles formed by a transversal cutting two parallel roads are in the ratio 3:2. Find the measures of these angles.
questions.explanation: Adjacent angles on a straight line sum to 180°. Let angles be 3x and 2x. Then 3x + 2x = 180 ⇒ 5x = 180 ⇒ x = 36. Angles are 108° and 72°.

questions.text: In the figure, two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. If one of the alternate interior angles is 3x + 10° and the corresponding angle to it is 5x - 14°, find the value of x and the measure of each angle.
questions.explanation: Since the lines are parallel, alternate interior angles are equal: 3x + 10 = 5x - 14. Solving, 2x = 24, x = 12°. Substitute x=12: angles = 3(12)+10=46°, and 5(12)-14=46°.

questions.text: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. The sum of one pair of co-interior angles is 130°. Find the measures of these angles and determine if the lines can be parallel. Justify your answer.

options.a: Angles are 65° and 65°, lines are parallel
options.b: Angles are 70° and 60°, lines are not parallel
options.c: Angles are 80° and 50°, lines are not parallel
options.d: Angles are 60° and 70°, lines are parallel

questions.explanation: Co-interior angles on parallel lines sum to 180°. Here, sum is 130°, which is less than 180°, so lines are not parallel. Angles can be 70° and 60°.

questions.text: In a figure, a transversal cuts two lines such that one corresponding angle is 4x - 5° and the alternate interior angle is 3x + 10°. If the lines are parallel, find the value of x and the measure of the angles.
questions.explanation: For parallel lines, corresponding angles equal alternate interior angles: 4x - 5 = 3x + 10. Solving, x = 15. Angles: 4(15)-5=55°, 3(15)+10=55°.

questions.text: A straight line is intersected by two parallel lines, forming four angles at the point of intersection. If one angle is (2x + 20)°, find the measures of all four angles and the value of x, given that one angle adjacent to it is (3x - 10)°.
questions.explanation: Adjacent angles on a straight line sum to 180°: (2x+20) + (3x-10) = 180. So 5x + 10 = 180, 5x = 170, x = 34 (closest option 30). Using x=30: angles are 2(30)+20=80°, 3(30)-10=80°, but options mismatch. Correct calculation: 2x+20 + 3x-10=180 => 5x+10=180 => 5x=170 => x=34. Angles: 2(34)+20=88°, 3(34)-10=92°. Vertically opposite angles equal: 88°, 92°, and their supplements 92°, 88°.

questions.text: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. If one of the interior angles on the same side of the transversal is (5x + 15)° and the other is (3x + 45)°, find x and the measure of each angle.
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles on same side sum to 180°: (5x+15) + (3x+45) = 180 => 8x + 60 =180 => 8x=120 => x=15. Substitute x=15: angles are 5(15)+15=90°, 3(15)+45=90°. So angles are equal 90° and 90°. Option d matches.

questions.text: In a figure, two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. If one of the vertically opposite angles is (7x - 20)° and its adjacent angle is (5x + 10)°, find the value of x and the measure of all angles formed at the intersection.

Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. If one of the alternate interior angles is 65°, find the measures of the corresponding angle and the co-interior angle on the same side of the transversal.
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal, so corresponding angle = 65°. Co-interior angles are supplementary, so co-interior angle = 180° - 65° = 115°.

questions.text: In a pair of vertically opposite angles, one angle measures (3x + 15)° and the other measures (5x - 25)°. Find the value of x and the measure of each angle.
accepted_forms: x = 20 and angles are 75 degrees each
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal: 3x + 15 = 5x - 25. Solving gives x = 20. Substituting x gives angle = 3(20)+15 = 75°.

questions.text: A transversal cuts two lines such that a pair of corresponding angles are supplementary. Are the two lines parallel? Justify your answer.
questions.correct_answer: No, the lines are not parallel because corresponding angles are equal when lines are parallel, not supplementary.
accepted_forms: No, lines are not parallel because corresponding angles are supplementary
No, because corresponding angles are equal only if lines are parallel
No, lines not parallel as corresponding angles must be equal
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles are equal if the lines are parallel. If they are supplementary, the lines are not parallel.

questions.text: If two adjacent angles form a linear pair and one angle is 110°, find the other angle. Also, state the property used.
questions.correct_answer: 70°; Angle sum property of a straight line
accepted_forms: 70 degrees; angle sum property of a straight line
questions.explanation: Angles forming a linear pair add up to 180°. So, other angle = 180° - 110° = 70°.

questions.text: In the figure, two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. If one of the co-interior angles is (2x + 10)° and the other is (3x - 20)°, find x and the measure of each angle.
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles are supplementary: (2x + 10) + (3x - 20) = 180. Solving gives x=30. Angles are 2(30)+10=70° and 3(30)-20=80°.

questions.text: If one angle of a complementary pair is (4x - 5)°, find the other angle in terms of x and the value of x when the other angle is 55°.
questions.explanation: Complementary angles sum to 90°, so other angle = 90° - (4x - 5) = (95 - 4x)°. Given other angle = 55°, so 95 - 4x = 55 ⇒ x=10.

questions.text: Two lines are cut by a transversal such that one pair of alternate interior angles are (3x + 20)° and (5x - 40)°. Find x and the measure of each angle. Are the lines parallel?
questions.correct_answer: x = 30; angles = 110° and 110°; Yes, lines are parallel
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal if lines are parallel: 3x + 20 = 5x - 40 ⇒ 2x=60 ⇒ x=30. Angles = 3(30)+20=110°. Hence lines are parallel.

questions.text: Find the measure of an angle if it is supplementary to its complement.
questions.explanation: Let angle be x. Its complement = 90 - x, supplementary = 180 - x. Given x is supplementary to its complement: x + (90 - x) = 180 ⇒ 90 = 180, contradiction unless angle = 90°.

questions.text: If the sum of two adjacent angles is 180° and one angle is twice the other, find the measures of the two angles.
questions.explanation: Let smaller angle = x, then larger = 2x. Sum = x + 2x = 3x = 180 ⇒ x=60. Angles are 60° and 120°.

questions.text: In a figure, a transversal cuts two lines and forms a pair of corresponding angles measuring (x + 25)° and (3x - 15)°. Find x and state whether the lines are parallel.
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles are equal if lines are parallel: x + 25 = 3x - 15 ⇒ 2x=40 ⇒ x=20. Since angles are equal, lines are parallel.

questions.case_text: In a busy Mumbai railway station, two railway tracks run parallel to each other. A footbridge crosses these tracks, acting as a transversal line. At one point, the angle formed between the footbridge and the first railway track is measured as 65°. The station manager wants to check the angles formed at the intersection of the footbridge with the second railway track to ensure safety signs are placed correctly.

subparts.text: What is the measure of the corresponding angle formed between the footbridge and the second railway track?
subparts.explanation: Corresponding angles formed by a transversal cutting two parallel lines are equal, so the angle is 65°.

subparts.text: If the angle between the footbridge and the first railway track is 65°, what is the measure of the alternate interior angle on the opposite side of the footbridge with respect to the first track?
subparts.explanation: Alternate interior angles between parallel lines cut by a transversal are equal, so the angle is 65°.

subparts.text: Calculate the measure of the angle adjacent to the 65° angle on the first railway track formed by the footbridge.
subparts.explanation: Adjacent angles on a straight line sum to 180°, so 180° - 65° = 115°.

questions.case_text: In a school playground in Delhi, two parallel boundary walls are connected by a ramp which acts as a transversal. The angle between the ramp and one boundary wall is 40°. The school principal wants to install lights at specific angles to illuminate the ramp properly at night and needs to know the related angle measures formed by the ramp with the other boundary wall.

subparts.text: What is the measure of the co-interior angle on the same side of the ramp with respect to the other boundary wall?
subparts.explanation: Co-interior angles on the same side of the transversal sum to 180°, so 180° - 40° = 140°.

subparts.text: If the angle between the ramp and one boundary wall is 40°, what is the measure of the vertically opposite angle at the intersection with the same boundary wall?
subparts.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal, so the angle is 40°.

subparts.text: Find the measure of the supplementary angle to the 40° angle formed by the ramp and the boundary wall.
